data.title: UMH Properties Buy Rating Reaffirmed as Growth Outlook Supports Unchanged $20.50 Price Target
data.description:
In a report released yesterday, Michael Diana from Maxim Group reiterated a Buy rating on Umh, with a price target of $20.50.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Michael Diana has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors, including solid cash flow coverage of the dividend and strong operating performance. UMH’s core FFO in 1Q26 fully supported its quarterly payout, while community-level NOI continued to grow at a rate above that of its manufactured housing peers, reflecting the success of its value-add acquisition and rental expansion strategy.

He also emphasizes the company’s embedded growth from thousands of vacant sites that can be profitably filled over the next several years, alongside a balance sheet and capital access that support continued expansion. With 2026 core FFO expected to rise nearly 10% year over year, UMH’s current valuation appears attractive relative to its growth profile, underpinning an unchanged $20.50 price target and the potential for a high-30% total return when combining yield and anticipated price appreciation.

In another report released yesterday, Colliers Securities also maintained a Buy rating on the stock with a $19.00 price target.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk

data.title: Analyst Maintains Hold on Tetra Tech as Softer 2026 Outlook and Slowing U.S. Public-Sector Demand Offset Strong Recent Results
data.description:
Maxim Group analyst Tate Sullivan has maintained their neutral stance on TTEK stock, giving a Hold rating on April 30.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Tate Sullivan has given his Hold rating due to a combination of factors tied to both recent performance and the forward outlook. While Tetra Tech delivered quarterly revenue and earnings that surpassed expectations and raised its full-year guidance, the company is still projecting year-over-year declines in revenue and EPS for fiscal 2026, reflecting a more challenging growth profile versus the prior year.

At the same time, Sullivan notes rising pressure on U.S. public-sector demand, including an anticipated slowdown in state and local market growth and reduced federal budget support and grant funding for water-related projects. Although Tetra Tech continues to generate solid cash flow, de-lever its balance sheet, and return capital through dividends and buybacks, these positives are balanced by the softer outlook, leading him to view the current valuation as fairly reflecting near-term risks and opportunities rather than warranting a more aggressive rating.

In another report released on April 30, Robert W. Baird also maintained a Hold rating on the stock with a $35.00 price target.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk

data.title: Analyst Maintains Buy on Healthcare REIT, Reaffirming $8 Price Target on FFO Growth and Attractive Yield
data.description:
Analyst Michael Diana from Maxim Group reiterated a Buy rating on Medical Properties Trust and keeping the price target at $8.00.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Michael Diana has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors, including solid first-quarter results and a visible path to higher cash flow. He highlights that normalized FFO is already comfortably covering the dividend, and that revenues are growing as repositioned, post-bankruptcy hospitals resume and increase rent payments in line with contractual terms.

He also underscores that management reaffirmed its target of at least $1 billion in annualized cash rent by the end of 2026, supported by built-in rent escalators and no need for significant new acquisitions. In his view, the stock’s current valuation multiple is too low relative to healthcare REIT peers, and maintaining an $8 price target reflects both the anticipated FFO ramp and an attractive total return when combined with the approximately 7% dividend yield.

In another report released on April 30, Colliers Securities also maintained a Buy rating on the stock with a $6.50 price target.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk

data.title: Analyst Lifts Nutex Price Target to $175 and Reiterates Buy on Expansion, Strong Cash Generation, and Upside Valuation
data.description:
Analyst Anthony Vendetti from Maxim Group maintained a Buy rating on Clinigence Holdings and increased the price target to $175.00 from $150.00.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Anthony Vendetti has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors that, in his view, outweigh the mixed nature of Nutex’s latest quarterly results. He highlights the company’s growing patient volumes across its micro-hospital network, ongoing expansion plans with multiple new facilities slated to open, and a stabilizing arbitration process that supports both reimbursement levels and cash collection trends.

He also points to Nutex’s solid balance sheet and operating cash generation as sufficient to fund its self-development strategy, which is expected to strengthen unit economics over time and reduce dependence on external developers. Combined with his upwardly revised revenue and EPS forecasts, and a discounted valuation versus peers that underpins a higher $175 price target, Vendetti believes the shares offer attractive upside potential, justifying his Buy recommendation.
